title: CSS Shorthand Properties Quiz
description: Test your knowledge of the essential shorthand properties for writing cleaner, more efficient, and smaller CSS files.

questions.question: Which of these is the most modern and recommended way to build a professional-grade web application?
options: Using CSS shorthand properties for margin/padding

questions.question: Which of these is a major 'Pro' of using CSS shorthand properties compared to writing them separately?
options: It makes your CSS files smaller and more readable.
It allows users to print only the core content.
It is always supported by every browser.
It replaces the need for any HTML5 tags.

questions.question: In a CSS margin shorthand rule (e.g., 'margin: 10px 20px 30px 40px;'), what is the order for the values?
Which shorthand property should you use to set the width, style, and color for an element edge all at once?
What is the primary danger of using shorthand properties if you aren't careful?
options: They make the site load much slower.
They can 'reset' any properties you didn't explicitly mention to their default values.
They are not supported in modern browsers.
They take up no space in the layout.
